title: As The Will Turns

jokes.text: If you xeroxed your butt again, I'm not interested.
jokes.setup: Will says something is 'huge' that they need to see
jokes.punchline: Phil's weary dismissal reveals this has happened before
jokes.explanation: Perfect character voice for Phil - tired authority figure dealing with Will's antics. Efficient setup/payoff with great specificity.

jokes.text: I hope it comes with a canopy because at that price, it's the only roof we'll have over our heads.
jokes.setup: Will complains about Lisa spending $1200 on a bed
jokes.punchline: Phil's dry observation about the financial implications
jokes.explanation: Classic Phil wit - dry, sophisticated humor about money troubles. Well-constructed but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: No, Philip, how are we?
jokes.setup: Phil makes sexist comment about women spending money
jokes.punchline: Viv's pointed correction calling out the generalization
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Viv calling out casual sexism. Sharp and economical.

jokes.text: I'm telling you, she's possessed. It's like some evil force has taken over her mind. Something not of this Earth.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Lisa's unusual spending
jokes.punchline: Will's increasingly dramatic supernatural explanation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure but fairly predictable 'women shopping' humor territory.

jokes.text: Ah! [Will's shocked reaction to Hilary arriving]
jokes.setup: Will just described Lisa being possessed by an evil force
jokes.punchline: Hilary appears immediately after, confirming his theory
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing gag. The horror movie scream after describing possession is well-executed physical comedy.

jokes.text: Cleopatra Jones.
jokes.setup: Will asks about his cousin who hosts the Hilary Banks Show
jokes.punchline: Worker gives completely wrong name that doesn't match Hilary at all
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection but the payoff is just a pop culture reference without much depth.

jokes.text: Oh, dag, that's your cousin? She's fine. Hey, tell Cleopatra Reggie said, 'What's up?'
jokes.setup: Worker realizes he's talking about Hilary
jokes.punchline: Still calls her Cleopatra and hits on her through Will
jokes.explanation: Decent callback but doesn't add much beyond extending the initial bit.

jokes.text: All right, but while you're down, can I get some dough out your wallet?
jokes.setup: Hilary says she thinks she's gonna faint from meeting her crush
jokes.punchline: Will's opportunistic request to raid her wallet while she's unconscious
jokes.explanation: Great character moment for Will - always opportunistic. Smart wordplay on 'dough' and excellent timing.

jokes.text: I'm pretty sure he's talking to you.
jokes.setup: Actor asks if Hilary is Hilary Banks, she giggles uncertainly
jokes.punchline: Will's dry clarification of the obvious
jokes.explanation: Functional joke playing off Hilary's ditzy character but nothing special.

jokes.text: What a fabulous butt.
jokes.setup: Actor asks Hilary if he can touch something
jokes.punchline: Director's comment seems to respond to inappropriate request but is about something else
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection timing but relies on crude humor. Effective but not elevated.

jokes.text: You know, it gets me around.
jokes.setup: Director compliments someone's butt
jokes.punchline: Will's casual acceptance of the compliment
jokes.explanation: Classic Will confidence and innuendo. In character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Hey, you can't treat an actor like that. What do you think he is, a writer?
jokes.setup: Will defending an actor being fired
jokes.punchline: Reveals the hierarchy by suggesting writers are treated worse
jokes.explanation: Sharp industry joke with great structure. Meta-humor about show business hierarchy is clever and quotable.

jokes.text: I mean, come on, man, the dude's a brother and he ain't supposed to be like, 'I'm looking for a Julius Caesar salad.' I mean, come on, man, you know, he got to be down here, you know. Hey, look, baby, you need the cops, we don't need to go down the station. All I got to do is walk out your front door carrying your TV, you know, okay, man.
jokes.setup: Will critiques the fired actor's performance
jokes.punchline: His 'improved' version is a stereotypical criminal character
jokes.explanation: Problematic racial humor that relies on stereotypes. Overly long and not particularly clever.

jokes.text: You're moving out? Even better. I'm moving out?
jokes.setup: Will says he has great news, Carlton guesses he's moving out
jokes.punchline: Carlton's excitement at the prospect, then confusion
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic between Will and Carlton. Shows their relationship through humor effectively.

jokes.text: Professor T. Johnson is a Troy? What did you think the T stood for? I was hoping Thelma.
jokes.setup: Reveal that Professor Johnson's first name is Troy
jokes.punchline: Phil's admission he hoped for a female name
jokes.explanation: Good setup of Phil's jealousy subplot. Character-driven humor that serves the story.

jokes.text: Oh, please. They hired you, somebody who's never acted in his life, to star in a network TV show. Pppt. Well, it happens.
jokes.setup: Carlton's incredulous dismissal of Will getting an acting job
jokes.punchline: Will's casual 'Well, it happens' response
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Will's confidence vs Carlton's skepticism.

jokes.text: Man, I ain't seen so many zeros since Carlton's square dance recital.
jokes.setup: Will looking at his contract with many zeros
jokes.punchline: Comparison to Carlton's embarrassing performance
jokes.explanation: Good insult that fits the Will/Carlton dynamic. Specific and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: What? You mean beside that little chocolate Tic Tac you call your head?
jokes.setup: Carlton points out Will is overlooking something
jokes.punchline: Will's creative insult about Carlton's head size
jokes.explanation: Creative and specific insult. 'Chocolate Tic Tac' is original and visual. Classic Will roasting Carlton.

jokes.text: Do I play a convincing coma victim or what?
jokes.setup: Will is lying motionless on the couch watching his soap opera performance
jokes.punchline: He's literally demonstrating his 'acting' while asking for validation
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-joke where Will demonstrates his 'skill' while critiquing it. Good visual comedy.

jokes.text: Well, you're no Sunny Von Bulow.
jokes.setup: Will asking about his coma performance
jokes.punchline: Carlton references famous real-life coma victim
jokes.explanation: Dark humor reference that fits Carlton's character. Clever but requires cultural knowledge.

jokes.text: I just hope that Sears lets them stay for the whole show.
jokes.setup: Will says his friends will watch on a big-screen TV
jokes.punchline: Implies they're watching in the store, not owning one
jokes.explanation: Good socioeconomic humor about Will's Philadelphia background. Character-appropriate.

jokes.text: [Will grunting] ...she have a hard time walking without falling over.
jokes.setup: Will mentions buying Lisa a big ring
jokes.punchline: Physical demonstration of Lisa being weighed down by the ring
jokes.explanation: Simple physical gag about expensive jewelry. Functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Not that they were in the same room or anything.
jokes.setup: Describing affair happening 'behind husband's back'
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of the phrase
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary literal-mindedness. Good character comedy showing her misunderstanding idioms.

jokes.text: That reminds me, Mrs. Banks called to say she'll be working late again...with Professor Johnson at her office writing her book.
jokes.setup: The soap opera plot about a cheating wife
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's perfectly timed delivery of parallel real-life situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony as the soap opera plot mirrors Phil's life. Geoffrey's timing would be impeccable.

jokes.text: I might as well be kissing Flavor Flav, gold teeth and all.
jokes.setup: Will explaining the kiss wasn't personal
jokes.punchline: Unflattering comparison to rapper Flavor Flav
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that fits Will's character. Specific and visual comparison.

jokes.text: So if I have to crawl into bed with 10 beautiful woman, that's just a sacrifice I'm gonna have to make.
jokes.setup: Will explaining he's only acting for Lisa's benefit
jokes.punchline: Framing romantic scenes as a 'sacrifice' he'll endure
jokes.explanation: Classic male character trying to have it both ways. Good character comedy showing Will's opportunism.

jokes.text: C, it's a prop.
jokes.setup: Carlton complains he can't flush the toilet
jokes.punchline: Simple explanation that it's not real
jokes.explanation: Simple gag about Carlton's naivety on set. Functional but basic.

jokes.text: [Makes squeaky noise instead of speaking]
jokes.setup: Will is supposed to deliver romantic dialogue
jokes.punchline: Makes a silly squeaking sound instead
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy undermining romantic scene. Silly but effective for disrupting soap opera tone.

jokes.text: And yet, inexplicably, we've had no leftovers.
jokes.setup: Phil notes Viv has missed dinner every night
jokes.punchline: Logical deduction about the missing food
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phil's logical mind and building his suspicion subplot.

jokes.text: If you were a woman, would you find me attractive? Nope.
jokes.setup: Phil's vulnerable question about his attractiveness
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's instant, brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey moment - brutal honesty delivered with no hesitation. Excellent timing and character consistency.

jokes.text: Why, he's 15 years her junior. Whatever would they do together?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey dismisses idea of Viv having an affair
jokes.punchline: Naive question that ignores the obvious
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Geoffrey's innocent nature vs Phil's suspicion.

jokes.text: What do you think of Larry? La-la-la Larry.
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions naming his Chia Pet
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic presentation of the mundane name 'Larry'
jokes.explanation: Pure Carlton absurdity - getting excited about naming a Chia Pet. Good character comedy.

jokes.text: Well, you seem like you're doing a pretty good job to me right now.
jokes.setup: Will claims he's not a good actor
jokes.punchline: Lisa points out he's convincingly acting worried about his acting
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-joke about acting within acting. Shows the paradox of Will's situation cleverly.

jokes.text: Oh... But this ain't one of them times.
jokes.setup: Will says he sometimes wants to hug Carlton
jokes.punchline: Clarifies this isn't one of those times
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's complicated feelings about Carlton. Classic misdirection.

jokes.text: So does watching it.
jokes.setup: Ashley says TV adds 10 pounds
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey suggests watching TV also adds weight
jokes.explanation: Clever twist on common phrase. Economical and observational humor about sedentary lifestyle.

jokes.text: Doesn't he know those stairs go nowhere?
jokes.setup: Director wants Carlton removed from set
jokes.punchline: Reveals Carlton is wandering on fake stairs
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about Carlton's naivety on a TV set. Functional setup for his character.

jokes.text: Actually, uh... We... We broke up.
jokes.setup: Soap opera scene about love
jokes.punchline: Will goes completely off-script with real information
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment where Will breaks character completely. Good setup for the chaos that follows.

jokes.text: No, no, like I told you, we broke up. You wanna dance?
jokes.setup: Co-star tries to get Will back on script
jokes.punchline: Will doubles down and makes it worse with random dance offer
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the disaster. Will's complete departure from script creates great chaos.

jokes.text: Now can we panic? / Cue dough boy. / I don't care if he's not ready, get him in there now, now, now!
jokes.setup: The situation gets worse as Will continues improvising
jokes.punchline: Complete production meltdown and desperate measures
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the production chaos. 'Dough boy' is a funny derogatory term for the backup actor.

jokes.text: I don't know.
jokes.setup: Derek asked what he's doing there
jokes.punchline: Actor admits he's completely lost
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective moment of actor confusion adding to the chaos. Honest and relatable.

jokes.text: That's right, I shot Carlos. And that's not all. I'm the one who shot J.R. too. And I shot the sheriff. But I did not shoot the deputy.
jokes.setup: Will confessing to murder in soap opera
jokes.punchline: Escalates through TV references to Bob Marley lyrics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from soap opera confession to pop culture references. The Bob Marley lyrics are perfect absurdist climax.

jokes.text: But you see, I'm young and I'm restless. I only got one life to live, so I got to follow my guiding light and search for tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Will explaining his motivation
jokes.punchline: String of soap opera title references
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor stringing together soap opera titles into coherent speech. Very clever writing.

jokes.text: Hey, who knows if I'll wind up living out the days of my life in a mansion in Dallas on the corner of Melrose Place drinking Falcon Crest out of a brown paper bag.
jokes.setup: Will continuing his rambling monologue
jokes.punchline: More TV show references ending with absurd image
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the meta-joke but slightly less clever than the previous one. Still very quotable.

jokes.text: The bald and the beautiful.
jokes.setup: Will professing love for the female character
jokes.punchline: References 'The Bold and the Beautiful' but changes it to 'bald'
jokes.explanation: Perfect cap to the soap opera titles sequence. Economical wordplay that works on multiple levels.

jokes.text: Got that right. / Isn't this great? / I told Daddy the soaps aren't predictable.
jokes.setup: Ashley's earlier claim about soap unpredictability
jokes.punchline: Her vindication after the most unpredictable soap episode ever
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to Ashley's earlier point, now ironically proven by Will's chaos rather than good writing.

jokes.text: [THUMP]
jokes.setup: Carlton demands Jerry give Will what's coming to him
jokes.punchline: Jerry literally punches Will instead of paying him
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection on 'give him what's coming to him.' Physical comedy payoff to the contract negotiation.

jokes.text: Oh, just go for it, sir. I'll swear it came that way.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey hands Phil an opened letter from Troy
jokes.punchline: Encourages Phil to read private mail and offers to lie about it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey moment - proper butler facade hiding mischievous nature. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Oh, I can't believe it either. He called her 'dear.'
jokes.setup: Phil expresses disbelief at the letter's contents
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey misinterprets what Phil finds shocking
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Geoffrey's different priorities. Character-appropriate humor.

jokes.text: That noodle-neck kid stomps on my last nerve.
jokes.setup: Phil asks about Professor Johnson
jokes.punchline: Viv's dismissive description of the young professor
jokes.explanation: 'Noodle-neck kid' is a creative insult that shows Viv's attitude. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Get a room.
jokes.setup: Phil and Viv embrace romantically
jokes.punchline: Will's standard response to PDA
jokes.explanation: Standard response to couple affection. Functional but very predictable and clichéd.

jokes.text: I haven't seen such a disaster on the telly since they cancelled Manimal.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey commenting on Will's TV performance
jokes.punchline: Reference to notorious short-lived TV show
jokes.explanation: Good TV industry reference that fits Geoffrey's sophisticated character. Clever comparison.

jokes.text: Please tell me you meant Chuck Berry.
jokes.setup: Hillary mentions Halle Berry will be on the show
jokes.punchline: Will hopes for the less attractive male musician instead
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will realizing what he's missing. Clever name confusion.

jokes.text: Ah! I knew there was something I forgot. [Will screaming and running]
jokes.setup: Will realizes he forgot to get his script for scenes with Halle Berry
jokes.punchline: Dramatic overreaction with screaming and running
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy ending showing Will's priorities. Typical sitcom ending with escalated reaction.

tmdb_overview: Will inadvertently finds himself in the right place at the right time and lands a part in a daytime soap opera.
